resume:
Director of Customer Support

Meridian Logistics — Director, Support Operations (2019-present). Lead a 70-person support org across two sites covering a B2B shipping platform. Own CSAT, time-to-resolution, and the support tooling budget.

Prior: Cobalt Retail — Support Manager (2015-2019). Ran a 20-agent team for an e-commerce returns desk.

Selected work: Replatformed the ticketing system; stood up a tier-2 escalation path; built the first QA scorecard for the team.

Skills: Zendesk admin, workforce scheduling, escalation management, hiring and coaching.

Education: BA Communications, Ohio State.
jd: Meridian Logistics seeks a Director of Customer Support to lead a 70-person organization serving freight and logistics customers across North America. You will own the operational metrics that the executive team watches weekly: CSAT (currently 81, target 88), median time-to-resolution (currently 9 hours, target 5), and first-contact resolution. You will manage a tooling and headcount budget, partner with Product on deflection and self-service, and be the escalation owner for the largest enterprise accounts. We are looking for a leader who can answer a direct operational question directly — when asked about a specific metric or decision, we expect a specific, on-point answer, not a tour of unrelated initiatives. This role reports to the COO and sits on the weekly operations review.

history.q: Walk me through the support org you run at Meridian — headcount, sites, and the metrics you own.
Your CSAT is sitting at 81 against a target of 88. Tell me specifically what's holding the score down and what you've done about it in the last quarter.

candidate_last_answer: You know, CSAT is interesting, but honestly what I'm most proud of is the culture we've built. We started doing these Friday team lunches and a peer-recognition program, and morale has never been higher. I also rolled out a new onboarding deck for new hires that's gotten great feedback, and we redesigned the break room. Oh, and we're piloting a four-day week experiment that I think is really the future of support. People love coming to work now.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: CSAT is interesting, but honestly what I'm most proud of is the culture

expected-yield: redirect the candidate back to the specific drivers of the 81-vs-88 CSAT gap and force a concrete diagnosis plus the actions taken last quarter, with at least one number, rather than the unrelated culture initiatives
